species: Sea Lion Colony Storm Day -- Steller's Sea Lion (Eumetopias jubatus) and/or California Sea Lion (Zalophus californianus) -- exposed rookery, roaring colony in rain and wind, winter storm
scientific_name: Eumetopias jubatus (Steller's Sea Lion, Family Otariidae) and/or Zalophus californianus (California Sea Lion, Family Otariidae). Exposed coastal rookery, Olympic Peninsula coast / offshore islands, winter storm conditions.
sound_type: Sea lion colony roaring in rain and wind on exposed coastal rookery during Pacific winter storm. Acoustic character: (1) Individual male Steller's bark: 100-800 Hz, peak 200-400 Hz, resonant and deep, carries over water; (2) Individual male California Sea Lion bark: 200-1,000 Hz, more nasal; (3) Females: 400-2,000 Hz contact and social calls; (4) Juveniles: 400-2,000 Hz bleats, grunts, contact calls; (5) Colony chorus: arrhythmic overlapping of hundreds of individuals, emergent acoustic texture transcending individual contribution; (6) Storm layer: wind on rock 50-400 Hz sustained, wave impact 10-100 Hz percussive irregular, rain distributed broadband 2,000-8,000 Hz. Sea lion colony low-to-mid (100-1,000 Hz) persists above storm broadband background. Colony does not quiet in the storm -- colony assertion intensifies. E=8 through collective acoustic assertion in adverse conditions.
ecosystem: Exposed coastal rookery -- sea stack, rocky island, or isolated headland on the Olympic Peninsula coast or offshore Washington. No lee shore protection. Full Pacific winter weather exposure. Wave-washed lower surfaces. Dense winter haul-out: 200-1,000+ mixed-age, mixed-sex animals. Primary WA sites: Protection Island (Jefferson County), Destruction Island (Jefferson County), Cape Flattery (Clallam County), offshore Olympic Peninsula rocks. Urban Puget Sound: California Sea Lions at Ballard Locks (Hiram Chittenden Locks, Seattle) year-round.
season: Winter -- storm season, non-breeding season haul-out. Pacific winter storms peak November through March. Colony density highest in winter (breeding not occurring; animals using site for rest, thermoregulation, social interaction). Steller's Sea Lions haul-out year-round; highest numbers in winter. California Sea Lions haul-out year-round in Washington.
family: Otariidae (eared seals). Steller's Sea Lion: largest Otariidae, adult male to 1,000+ kg, 3 m body length, ESA Threatened (Eastern North Pacific). California Sea Lion: adult male 200-400 kg, not ESA listed, population expanding post-MMPA 1972.
milestone: SECOND STEP OF THE FINAL ASCENT. E=8 base holds at degrees 341 (elk+storm+eagle, E=8) and 342 (sea lion colony storm, E=8) before rising to E=9 at 343 (peregrine through tern colony) and E=10 at 344 (multi-species polyrhythmic apex -- FIRST E=10 IN SPS ENGINE HISTORY). E=8 through collective acoustic assertion against adverse conditions: colony does not quiet in the storm; colony gets louder. Different mechanism from SPS 341 (three-stream convergence) -- single-source colony at maximum output amplified by storm framing.

coordinates.description: Representative: Cape Flattery area, Clallam County, WA. 48.38°N 124.72°W. Most northwestern point of contiguous US. Exposed to full North Pacific weather. Steller's Sea Lion haul-out sites on Tatoosh Island and adjacent offshore rocks. Destruction Island (47.67°N 124.49°W) and Protection Island (48.13°N 122.92°W) also primary WA sea lion haul-out sites. Urban reference: Ballard Locks / Hiram Chittenden Locks, Seattle (47.66°N 122.40°W) -- California Sea Lion year-round presence.

acoustic_data.steller_bark: Adult male: 100-800 Hz, peak 200-400 Hz, resonant deep bark. 2-3 seconds per bark. Single male at 10m: acoustically overwhelming. Colony of 100+ males: dense arrhythmic overlapping chorus with emergent texture.
acoustic_data.california_bark: Adult male: 200-1,000 Hz, more nasal quality. Recognized as 'arf-arf-arf' pattern. Carries over water to 1+ km from large individual. Colony chorus similar emergent property to Steller's.
acoustic_data.female_juvenile: Female: 400-2,000 Hz, contact and social calls, shorter duration. Juvenile: 400-2,000 Hz bleats, grunts, contact calls. Full colony includes all ages and sexes -- complete Otariidae acoustic range 100-2,000 Hz.
acoustic_data.storm_interaction: Wind on exposed rock: 50-400 Hz sustained, modulated by rock surface geometry. Wave impact on colony base: 10-100 Hz percussive irregular. Rain on colony surface: broadband 2,000-8,000 Hz distributed. Sea lion colony low-mid (100-1,000 Hz) remains audible above storm broadband; mist/spray preferentially attenuates high frequencies, preserving low-frequency colony vocalizations at range. Colony assertion continues and intensifies in storm conditions.
acoustic_data.e8_calibration: E=8 through collective assertion against adverse conditions. Single sea lion: E=4-5. Colony of 200-1,000+ in winter storm: E=8 through emergent acoustic density plus storm framing. Mechanism distinct from SPS 341 (three-stream convergence) and SPS 340 (concentration). The colony is an acoustic organism that exceeds its individual components.

nodes.title: Colony Assertion -- Roaring Against the Storm
nodes.description: The sea lion colony does not quiet in the Pacific winter storm. It asserts louder. 200-1,000+ individuals vocalizing simultaneously on an exposed rock, competing with wind (50-400 Hz) and wave impact (10-100 Hz) with their low-frequency barks (100-1,000 Hz). E=8 through collective assertion.

nodes.title: Emergent Acoustic Texture -- Colony Exceeds Sum of Individuals
nodes.description: A single sea lion: E=4-5. The colony at E=8 is not additive -- it is an emergent acoustic phenomenon arising from hundreds of individuals vocalizing simultaneously with arrhythmic overlap, social responses, and spatial distribution across the rookery surface. The colony is an acoustic organism.

nodes.title: Storm Framing -- Adverse Conditions Amplify the Colony
nodes.description: The Pacific winter storm does not suppress the sea lion colony's acoustic impact -- it frames it. Storm broadband (50-8,000 Hz) provides the background against which the colony's structured low-mid frequency vocalizations (100-1,000 Hz) are distinguishable. The storm is the contrast that makes the colony louder.

nodes.title: Final Ascent Step 2 -- E=8 Base Holds
nodes.description: SPS ramp: 341 (E=8, three-stream convergence) → 342 (E=8, colony assertion) → 343 (E=9, predation drama) → 344 (E=10, imagined synthesis). The base holds for two degrees. Two different mechanisms produce E=8 at 341 and 342: convergence vs. collective assertion.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 + E=8 = E=16. SECOND STEP FINAL ASCENT. Collective power against adversity: Gruntruck (four musicians at maximum metal commitment in difficult commercial conditions) + sea lion colony (hundreds of animals asserting against the storm). Both reach E=8 through collective assertion.
341 E=8 through three-stream spectral convergence. 342 E=8 through single-source colony assertion amplified by storm. Two consecutive E=8 base entries with different acoustic mechanisms.

E=8 rises to E=9 at 343. Colony at rest (342) vs. colony in predation event (343). The sea lion colony roars continuously; the tern colony explodes into chaos when the Peregrine stoops. Continuous assertion vs. explosive drama.
